text:
Jeet (ジート, Jīto) was a player of the Greed Island game. He was the first candidate to show Tsezguerra his skills and also the first to pass the recruitment test. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 125 

Appearance

Jeet was a tall, muscular man with black spiky hair. He wore a beige shirt in the 1999 anime adaptation, while in the 2011 anime adaptation, he wore a red shirt tucked into a pair of blue jeans.

Personality

Not much is known about Jeet's personality, aside from the fact that he was eager to go first for Tsezguerra's Ren test and smirks when he sees Gon and Killua in Antokiba. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 126 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Jeet is the first candidate to show his Ren to Tsezguerra and, along with Gon and Killua, is one of the 21 candidates hired by the billionaire Battera to complete Greed Island. Jeet and the other players all gather within Battera's mansion to be transferred into the game. 

Soon after entering the game, he and several other players observe the local Antokiba Tournament Billboard. Afterwards, he is found dead in the center of town. Jeet's death was due to an explosion to his abdomen from someone called the Bomber. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 130 Biscuit briefly mentions Jeet when she informs Gon and Killua that they have escaped death twice since starting the game. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 138 

Later, Jeet's name is listed in Gon's binder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Jeet was capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 1999 anime adaptation, an extra scene plays out involving Jeet cutting ahead of Biscuit Krueger when the participants of Tsezguerra's Ren test begin lining up to take it. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 73 (1999) 

Miscellaneous

Jeet shares the same name as the character Jeet from One Piece.
